label: 300d–44 General provisions

Effective DateSection effective , with programs making awards providing financial assistance in fiscal year 1993 and subsequent years effective for awards made on or after , see section 801(b), (d)(1) of , set out as an Effective Date of 1992 Amendment note under .July 10, 1992Oct. 1, 1992Pub. L. 102–321section 236 of this title

children.heading: Application
children.label: a Application
children.text: The Secretary may not award a grant to a trauma center under unless such center submits an application for the grant to the Secretary and the application is in such form, is made in such manner, and contains such agreements, assurances, and information as the Secretary determines to be necessary to carry out this part.section 300d–41(a) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on duration of support
children.label: b Limitation on duration of support
children.text: The period during which a trauma center receives payments under a grant under shall be for 3 fiscal years, except that the Secretary may waive such requirement for a center and authorize such center to receive such payments for 1 additional fiscal year.section 300d–41(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on amount of grant
children.label: c Limitation on amount of grant
children.text: Notwithstanding , a grant under may not be made in an amount exceeding $2,000,000 for each fiscal year.section 300d–42(a) of this titlesection 300d–41 of this title

children.heading: Eligibility
children.label: d Eligibility
children.text: Except as provided in , acquisition of, or eligibility for, a grant under shall not preclude a trauma center from being eligible for other grants described in such section.section 300d–42(b)(1)(B)(iii) of this titlesection 300d–41(a) of this title

children.heading: Funding distribution
children.label: e Funding distribution
children.text: Of the total amount appropriated for a fiscal year under , 70 percent shall be used for substantial uncompensated care awards under , 20 percent shall be used for core mission awards under , and 10 percent shall be used for emergency awards under .section 300d–45 of this titlesection 300d–41(a)(1) of this titlesection 300d–41(a)(2) of this titlesection 300d–41(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Minimum allowance
children.label: f Minimum allowance
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (e), if the amount appropriated for a fiscal year under is less than $25,000,000, all available funding for such fiscal year shall be used for substantial uncompensated care awards under .section 300d–45 of this titlesection 300d–41(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Substantial uncompensated care award distribution and proportional share
children.label: g Substantial uncompensated care award distribution and proportional share
A
children.text: 50 percent of such funds for category A trauma center grantees;

children.label: B
children.text: 35 percent of such funds for category B trauma center grantees; and

children.label: C
children.text: 15 percent of such funds for category C trauma center grantees; and
provide available funds within each category in a manner proportional to the award basis specified in to each eligible trauma center.section 300d–42(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Report
children.label: h Report
children.text: Beginning 2 years after , and every 2 years thereafter, the Secretary shall biennially report to Congress regarding the status of the grants made under and on the overall financial stability of trauma centers.March 23, 2010section 300d–41 of this title
